title: Cell structures and their functions
description: Test your knowledge of cell structures and their functions!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements about cell structures best supports the common ancestry of all living things?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All cells contain ribosomes.
Eukaryotes have nuclei and prokaryotes do not.
All plant cells have a cell wall.
Both plant and animal cells have mitochondria.

assessment_items.question_content:
The diagram below represents a rough endoplasmic reticulum. 


[[☃ image 1]]

 **Which of the following best describes the function of the structures labeled X?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The image shows flattened stacks with small purple circles all along the outer edges of the stacks. There are two arrows pointing at the small purple circles and the arrows are labeled X.

choices.content: They translate mRNA into amino acid sequences.
They contain enzymes that digest unnecessary or damaged cell parts.
They package proteins for export out of the cell.
They are where template DNA is transcribed to mRNA.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements is true for both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Ribosomes synthesize proteins using mRNA sequences.
Mitochondria produce ATP through cellular respiration.
The rough endoplasmic reticulum synthesizes lipids and modifies proteins.
The Golgi complex packages and sorts proteins for transport throughout the cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
A cytologist uses a microscope to compare a human hepatocyte and a human epithelial cell. She observes that the smooth endoplasmic reticulum (ER) of the hepatocyte is larger and contains more folds than the smooth ER of the epithelial cell.

**Which of the following is best supported by this observation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The hepatocyte is able to synthesize lipids more efficiently than the epithelial cell.
The hepatocyte is able to produce more proteins than the epithelial cell.
The epithelial cell is able to detoxify substances more efficiently than the hepatocyte.
The epithelial cell is able to generate more ATP than the hepatocyte.

assessment_items.question_content:
A damaged cell is unable to properly modify proteins after they are synthesized, which causes the proteins to misfold.

**Which of the following structures is most likely damaged in the cell?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Endoplasmic reticulum
Mitochondrion
Chloroplast
Ribosome

assessment_items.question_content:
The endoplasmic reticulum (ER) is a structure found in eukaryotic cells. It is composed of two parts: the smooth ER and the rough ER.

**Which of the following best describes a difference between the rough ER and the smooth ER?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rough ER has ribosomes on its surface, but the smooth ER does not.
The smooth ER is a site of protein synthesis, but the rough ER is not.
The rough ER processes toxins, but the smooth ER does not.
The smooth ER is part of the endomembrane system, but the rough ER is not.

assessment_items.question_content:
Eukaryotic cells contain an intricate endomembrane system that includes the nucleus, endoplasmic reticulum (ER), and Golgi complex. The Golgi complex and rough ER are often near each other, which is essential for their roles in the endomembrane system.

**Which of the following most accurately explains why the Golgi complex is often near the rough ER?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Golgi complex modifies and packages proteins that are produced in the rough ER.
The Golgi complex translates proteins using instructions provided by the rough ER.
The Golgi complex modifies lipids that have been produced by the rough ER.
The Golgi complex uses enzymes to digest misfolded proteins that are produced in the rough ER.

assessment_items.question_content:
A cell is treated with a toxin that prevents the synthesis of new lysosomes. However, the cell continues to produce the hydrolytic enzymes normally found in lysosomes.

**In which of the following structures are the hydrolytic enzymes most likely to accumulate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Golgi complex
Mitochondria
Smooth endoplasmic reticulum
Nucleus

assessment_items.question_content:
Leigh syndrome is a rare genetic disorder that affects the central nervous system. Most individuals with Leigh syndrome have mitochondria that cannot meet the energy demands of the cell. 

**Which of the following is the most likely outcome of Leigh syndrome on affected mitochondria?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The mitochondria cannot produce sufficient ATP to power normal cellular processes.
The mitochondria cannot transcribe DNA in preparation for protein synthesis.
The mitochondria cannot produce enough glucose molecules for the cell.
The mitochondria cannot process and package proteins for further transport.

assessment_items.question_content:
A student uses a microscope to compare a human skeletal muscle cell and a human epithelial cell. The student observes that the muscle cell has more mitochondria than the epithelial cell.

**Which of the following conclusions is best supported by this observation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The muscle cell requires more ATP than the epithelial cell.
The epithelial cell requires more ATP than the muscle cell.
The epithelial cell does not require ATP to function.
The muscle cell does not require ATP to function.

assessment_items.question_content:
Phenytoin is a prescription drug that is used to treat patients with epilepsy. A side effect of phenytoin is that it can be toxic to mitochondria.

**Which of the following processes will be most immediately disrupted by this side effect?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ATP synthesis
Protein synthesis
Lipid synthesis
Lysosome synthesis

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how the internal environment of a lysosome compares to the environment of the cytosol?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The internal environment of a lysosome is more acidic than the cytosol.
The internal environment of a lysosome has a higher pH than the cytosol.
The internal environment of a lysosome is surrounded by a membrane and the cytosol is not.
The internal environment of a lysosome contains ribosomes and the cytosol does not.

assessment_items.question_content:
Tay-Sachs disease is a genetic disorder that causes certain lipids known as gangliosides to accumulate in the neurons of the brain and spinal cord. As the gangliosides build up, neurons rapidly degenerate.

**Which of the following cell structures is most likely associated with the accumulation of gangliosides?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Lysosomes
Mitochondria
Endoplasmic reticulum
Nucleus

assessment_items.question_content:
Wolman disease affects lysosomal function and results in the accumulation of lipids in the liver, spleen, intestines, adrenal glands, and lymph nodes.

**Which of the following best explains why excess lipids accumulate in individuals with Wolman disease?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The lysosomes contain an insufficient amount of lipid-digesting enzymes.
The lysosomes accumulate extra lipid-digesting enzymes.
The lysosomes allow excess lipids to enter the cell through endocytosis.
The lysosomes prevent lipids from entering the cell through endocytosis.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how the central vacuole helps support the structure of a plant cell?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When the central vacuole is full of water, it applies pressure to the cell wall and maintains the shape of the cell.
The central vacuole uses a network of protein fibers to support the shape of the plant cell.
The central vacuole uses a large, rigid membrane to support the shape of the plant cell.
When the central vacuole is empty, it expands in order to fill space and maintain the cell’s structure.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how vacuoles differ between plant cells and animal cells?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Plant cells typically have a single vacuole while animal cells have multiple vacuoles.
Animal cells use vacuoles for nutrient storage while plant cells do not.
Vacuoles in animal cells provide turgor pressure to help the cell maintain its shape.
Vacuoles in plant cells are small and used to transport nutrients throughout the cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
A lack of water can cause plant cells to become flaccid. This gives the plant a wilted appearance as shown in the figure below. 


[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following cell structures is most likely responsible for the plant’s wilted appearance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A drawing of a flowering plant with the stems bent over and leaves falling off of the plant.

choices.content: Central vacuole
Mitochondria
Nucleus
Ribosomes

assessment_items.question_content:
Several botanists are studying the effect of high temperature on chloroplast function in *Arabidopsis thaliana*, a species of flowering plant. The botanists find that extremely high temperatures damage the membrane of the chloroplasts. This causes the contents of the chloroplasts to leak into the cytosol, which makes them nonfunctional.

**Which of the following is a likely immediate consequence of this damage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Glucose production in the cell will decrease.
ATP production in the cell will decrease.
The chloroplast cannot transcribe DNA for protein synthesis.
The chloroplast cannot synthesize lipids for the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
Chlorophyll *a* is a pigment that absorbs light energy from the sun and gives plants their green color.

**Which of the following plant cell structures is most likely to contain chlorophyll *a*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Chloroplast
Mitochondria
Vacuole
Ribosome

assessment_items.question_content:
An unknown cell is determined to contain a nucleus, a cell wall, mitochondria, chloroplasts, and ribosomes.

**Which of the following statements about the unknown cell is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The unknown cell can perform photosynthesis.
The unknown cell is a prokaryote.
The unknown cell is an animal cell.
The unknown cell cannot synthesize proteins.
